authors: By Clémence Guetté, Hadrien Clouet and Jean-Luc Mélenchon
content.0.paragraphs.0: The Future in Common is presented first and foremost as an electoral program. It has been drafted, updated, and revised for various presidential elections. Its starting point is the Human First program, championed by La France Insoumise and the Communists in the 2012 presidential election. Then, in its new form, it became the platform of La France Insoumise in the 2017 and 2022 presidential campaigns. Based on this, the programs of the Popular Union were subsequently written for the 2022 and 2024 legislative elections. Thus, an electoral bloc of several million people, mobilized in favor of a clean break with the neoliberal world, has gradually formed around this text.
content.0.paragraphs.1: If he was able to achieve this, it was thanks to the method used to select his 831 measures across all areas of national policy for a nation like France. The program originated from society itself: associations, unions, collectives, and scientists. In fact, everything was already produced based on concrete needs identified through experience and popularized by their mobilization campaigns. These booklets of demands, reports, and transcripts of hearings are the original and ever-renewed raw material of L’Avenir en Commun (The Future in Common). In 2020 and 2021, when it became necessary to prepare for the 2022 presidential election, two public consultations were organized, resulting in over 15,000 individual contributions and thematic hearings with stakeholders from organized society.
